rxn_class_summary: Photoredox/pyridine N-oxide co-catalyzed regioselective carbohydroxylation and aminohydroxylation of unactivated alpha-olefins with water as the hydroxyl source

key_intermediates.name: tertiary carbon-centered radical from anti-Markovnikov N-oxy radical addition to alpha-olefin (Int1)
Giese adduct radical with benzalmalononitrile (post C-C bond formation)
stabilized malononitrile carbanion (Int3) after SET reduction by PC2-reduced
N-alkoxypyridinium trifluoroacetate intermediate (Int4) after protonation by TFA

electron_flow: Photoexcited acridinium PC2* performs single-electron oxidation of pyridine N-oxide 1b to generate an electrophilic N-oxy radical, which adds to the terminal carbon of the alpha-olefin to give a more substituted C-radical; this radical undergoes Giese addition to benzalmalononitrile (or DIAD), and the resulting adduct radical is reduced by PC2-radical-anion (regenerating PC2) to a carbanion that is protonated by TFA, forming an N-alkoxypyridinium that undergoes SN-type substitution by water (TFA-activated) to deliver the primary alcohol and regenerate pyridine N-oxide.
rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic substitution of pyridine N-oxide from the N-alkoxypyridinium by water (TS_Sub, DFT free-energy barrier 24.3 kcal/mol), with water activated by trifluoroacetate counterion

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O (95 atom% 18O)
probe_reagents.observation: Carbohydroxylation of 2-ethyl-1-butene gave primary alcohol with 18O/16O = 92:8 (78% yield), confirming water as the hydroxyl source via nucleophilic substitution of N-alkoxypyridinium

probe_reagents.name: 1,6-heptadiene (radical clock / intramolecular trap)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclization/carbohydroxylation gave disubstituted cyclopentane 32 in 52% yield, evidencing a discrete carbon radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Stern-Volmer fluorescence quenching with 1b
probe_reagents.observation: Excited PC2* quenched by 2,6-dichloropyridine N-oxide 1b (Ksv = 34.2) but not appreciably by alpha-olefin (Ksv = 1.8) or benzalmalononitrile, supporting initial SET to 1b

probe_reagents.name: tert-butanol (alternative nucleophile)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave carboetherification product 33 (42%), trapping the N-alkoxypyridinium electrophile

probe_reagents.name: acetic acid / acetate buffer
probe_reagents.observation: Gave carboesterification product 34 (56%), consistent with polar substitution on N-alkoxypyridinium

probe_reagents.name: 4-pentenoic acid (intramolecular carboxylate)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivered gamma-substituted delta-lactone 35, proof-of-principle for cyclization via N-alkoxypyridinium substitution

notes: Two pathways were considered for alcohol formation from the N-alkoxypyridinium: (favored) water nucleophilic substitution regenerating pyridine N-oxide (supported by catalytic 1b loading and 18O labeling), vs (alternative) reductive N-O bond cleavage which would consume 1b stoichiometrically. Competing ortho-alkylation of pyridine N-oxide (product 3) and allylic C-H functionalization (product 4 with 2,6-dibromopyridine N-oxide 1d; product 35 from cyclohexene) are side pathways suppressed by using ortho-chlorinated 1b. Direct SET oxidation of the alpha-olefin is excluded because olefin oxidation potential (>+2.50 V vs SCE) exceeds PC2* (+2.15 V).
